query: Florida 2026 gubernatorial GOP primary debate schedule Byron Donalds James Fishback David Jolly - any debates scheduled or held summer 2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the Florida 2026 GOP gubernatorial primary debate situation:

## Summary

**Sanctioned debates: None held or scheduled.** As of July 11, 2026, no party-sanctioned head-to-head GOP primary debate has occurred, and none is likely before the **August 18, 2026** primary. The reason: Trump-endorsed frontrunner **Byron Donalds** is the only candidate meeting RPOF's "10/10/10" criteria (≥10% polling, >$10M raised, >10,000 donors), and he refuses to debate ("no participation trophies"; will only debate once a rival hits "double digits").

**An unsanctioned "undercard" debate DID happen (early July 2026):** "The Future of Florida" livestream on YouTube, hosted by podcaster **Patrick Bet-David**, featuring **Jay Collins, James Fishback, and Paul Renner**. Donalds skipped it. ~2 hours, ~7,000 peak viewers, no broadcast/cable network aired it. Marked by a heated Collins–Fishback clash over a ballot-eligibility lawsuit.

**Other debate-related timeline points:**
- **June 27, 2026** – RPOF Sunshine State Showdown (Hollywood/Davie): candidates appeared individually, but no head-to-head debate. Fishback was disinvited for defying party rules.
- **July 15, 2026** – Fishback announced a separate unsanctioned CBS-affiliate debate in Jacksonville ("whether Byron Donalds shows or not").
- **June 13, 2026** – Fishback confronted Donalds with a megaphone in Lake City demanding a debate.
- Gov. DeSantis publicly joined calls for a debate, criticizing party leadership.

**On David Jolly:** He is the likely **Democratic** nominee — not a GOP primary participant. Reporting anticipates an eventual Donalds–Jolly general-election debate.

Full structured findings (key facts, data points, and sources) have been recorded.

key_facts: Florida GOP gubernatorial primary is August 18, 2026; general election November 3, 2026.
No party-sanctioned GOP primary debate has been held or scheduled as of July 11, 2026, because only Byron Donalds meets RPOF debate criteria and he refuses to debate.
RPOF 'Sunshine State Showdown' held June 27, 2026 at Seminole Hard Rock Hotel & Casino (Hollywood/Davie); candidates appeared individually but NO head-to-head debate occurred.
RPOF's 10/10/10 debate criteria (released mid-June 2026): >=10% in RPOF poll, >$10M raised, >10,000 donors; only Donalds qualified.

An unsanctioned 'undercard' debate — 'The Future of Florida' — was livestreamed on YouTube (hosted by podcaster Patrick Bet-David / PBD Podcast) in early July 2026, featuring Jay Collins, James Fishback, and Paul Renner. Donalds skipped it.
The Bet-David debate ran ~2 hours, drew ~7,000 peak YouTube viewers (~1,200 concurrent per one count), and mentioned Donalds by name 60+ times. No broadcast/cable network agreed to air it.

James Fishback announced an unsanctioned CBS-affiliate debate in Jacksonville for July 15, 2026 ('whether Byron Donalds shows or not'); RPOF rescinded his Sunshine State Showdown invitation for defying party rules.
On June 13, 2026, Fishback confronted Donalds with a megaphone outside a Lake City campaign event demanding a debate.

Donalds's stated position: he'd only debate once another candidate reaches 'double digits'; he cites ~$100M raised and 48-58% polling. Told WPLG 'If the race was close... we would be debating right now.'
David Jolly is the likely DEMOCRATIC nominee (former U.S. Rep.), not a GOP primary debate participant; reporting expects a Donalds-Jolly general-election debate.
Gov. Ron DeSantis joined candidates in calling for a public GOP debate, criticizing party leadership.

Lt. Gov. Jay Collins sued Fishback (and Florida Division of Elections) over Fishback's residency/eligibility; a Leon County judge set aside two July days to rule on whether Fishback stays on the ballot (fast-tracked as of July 7, 2026).
Polymarket priced Donalds ~95.6% to win the GOP primary as of July 11, 2026.
